skill.name: wan-2-7
skill.description: Guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through generating short Wan 2.7 text-to-video clips with the RunComfy CLI, including prompt structure, audio lip-sync settings, output options, and model limitations.
skill.summary: Generate Wan 2.7 text-to-video clips through RunComfy with guided prompt, audio, and output settings.
skill.author: doany-ai

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, network, filesystem, and blocker patterns. Review found most command and blocker detections are markdown examples or false positives, but the skill intentionally uses the RunComfy CLI, sends prompts and media references to RunComfy endpoints, and stores or reads an API token. No prompt injection attempt or confirmed malicious behavior was found.

medium_findings.title: RunComfy CLI Executes Local Commands and Submits Remote Jobs
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to run the local RunComfy CLI and submit text-to-video jobs to RunComfy. This is expected for the skill, but it creates external command execution and third-party processing risk for prompts and media references.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill explicitly documents running RunComfy commands and describes POST requests to RunComfy model API endpoints. The behavior appears intentional and not malicious, but it is a real execution and network boundary.

medium_findings.title: API Token Stored in User Configuration or Environment
medium_findings.description: The skill documents RunComfy authentication through a local token file or RUNCOMFY_TOKEN. This is normal CLI behavior, but token storage and environment-variable use require care to avoid credential exposure.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file directly names the token path and environment variable. The finding is medium rather than high because the text also states owner-only file permissions and does not show exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded RunComfy URLs and Example Media URL
low_findings.description: The skill contains RunComfy documentation links, model API hostnames, and an example audio URL placeholder. These are expected for a hosted video-generation workflow, but users should treat third-party URLs as external data flows.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible and tied to the documented RunComfy service. No evidence found of hidden callbacks, telemetry, or unrelated destinations.

low_findings.title: Static High-Risk Pattern Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography, system reconnaissance, and path traversal detections do not match semantic behavior in the reviewed context. They appear to come from ordinary prose, table text, or the placeholder URL with ellipsis.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Review of the cited lines found model description, routing guidance, a placeholder URL, an anti-pattern heading, and an exit-code heading. No evidence found of cryptographic logic, host reconnaissance, or real traversal.

dangerous_patterns.title: Documented External Command Invocation
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill provides command examples for installing and running RunComfy. These are legitimate instructions, but marketplace users should understand that the skill asks the agent to invoke a local CLI.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit shell examples in markdown. They use a known CLI and structured input, so the pattern is real but not evidence of malicious intent.

dangerous_patterns.title: Third-Party Model API Data Flow
dangerous_patterns.description: Prompts, optional audio URLs, and generated output downloads are handled by RunComfy infrastructure. This is central to the skill, but it can expose user content to an external processor.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The skill explicitly describes the endpoint, polling, download behavior, and allowed output hosts. The risk is clear, but scoped to the documented service.

content.value_statement: Creating short AI video clips requires precise model settings, prompt structure, and output handling. This skill guides Wan 2.7 requests through RunComfy with clear defaults, audio options, and known limits.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to choose Wan 2.7 instead of related video models.
Documents the RunComfy text-to-video endpoint and supported input fields.
Shows CLI commands for basic generation, lip-sync audio, and literal prompt control.
Defines supported duration, resolution, aspect ratio, seed, and negative prompt options.
Provides prompt guidance for camera movement, primary action, and audio matching.
Summarizes output handling, exit codes, token storage, and network endpoints.
limitations: Requires the RunComfy CLI, account access, and a valid token.
Generates clips up to 15 seconds with a 1080p maximum resolution.
Does not create in-pass voice audio; users must provide audio for lip-sync.
Sends prompts and media references to RunComfy for remote processing.

use_cases.title: Create Product Video Tests
use_cases.description: Generate short product showcase clips with controlled camera motion, aspect ratio, and negative prompts.

use_cases.title: Produce Lip-Synced Social Clips
use_cases.description: Use an existing voiceover URL with Wan 2.7 to create vertical spokesperson or announcement videos.

use_cases.title: Prototype Motion Concepts
use_cases.description: Compare seeds, prompt expansion, and literal prompts before committing to a video direction.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Text-to-Video Clip
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a short generated clip
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Wan 2.7 on RunComfy to generate a 5 second 1080p 16:9 video from this idea: [describe the scene].

prompt_templates.title: Controlled Product Shot
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request with camera and negative prompt details
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a Wan 2.7 product video with a slow camera move, one clear action, 1080p output, and negative prompts for flicker, subtitles, and watermark.

prompt_templates.title: Lip-Synced Vertical Video
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 9:16 Wan 2.7 clip using this audio URL for lip-sync: [audio URL]. Match the duration and keep the camera locked on the speaker.

prompt_templates.title: Seeded Variant Workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced iteration across several controlled outputs
prompt_templates.prompt: Run Wan 2.7 variants for this prompt using the same seed first, then change only lighting, camera distance, and negative prompt terms.

output_examples.input: A user asks for a five second premium product video in landscape format.
output_examples.output: The assistant prepares a RunComfy Wan 2.7 request with a concise product prompt, 1080p resolution, 16:9 aspect ratio, and an output directory.

output_examples.input: A user provides a short voiceover URL and asks for a social media spokesperson clip.
output_examples.output: The assistant checks audio duration requirements, uses 9:16 output, includes the audio URL, and keeps the prompt focused on one speaking subject.

output_examples.input: A user wants exact wording and minimal prompt changes for a brand-sensitive video.
output_examples.output: The assistant disables prompt expansion, keeps the wording literal, and uses a targeted negative prompt for unwanted artifacts.

best_practices: Use one primary action per clip and describe camera motion early.
Match audio length to the requested duration before using lip-sync.
Reuse seeds for controlled variants and change seeds for broader exploration.
anti_patterns: Do not submit secrets, private customer data, or confidential media URLs as prompts.
Do not combine many unrelated actions in one short clip request.
Do not expect 4K output, long scenes, or unsupported aspect ratios.

faq.question: What does this skill generate?
faq.answer: It helps generate short Wan 2.7 text-to-video clips through the RunComfy CLI.

faq.question: Does it require a RunComfy account?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow requires the RunComfy CLI and authentication through login or RUNCOMFY_TOKEN.

faq.question: Can it make lip-synced videos?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports audio-driven lip-sync when the user provides a compatible audio_url.

faq.question: What are the main output limits?
faq.answer: The documented limits are 15 second duration, 1080p maximum resolution, and five supported aspect ratios.

faq.question: Does the skill process video locally?
faq.answer: No. It submits jobs to RunComfy infrastructure and downloads generated outputs.

faq.question: Is it safe to use with private prompts?
faq.answer: Use caution. Prompts and media references are sent to a third-party service for processing.
